summary: User policy for Windows Spotlight in Action Center or notification surfaces. Good for a quieter notification area, but it removes Spotlight suggestions and prompts in that space.

setting.name: Windows Spotlight on Action Center
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show Spotlight-driven suggestions or prompts in Action Center style notification surfaces. This policy decides whether that content is allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to reduce notification-area clutter and stop Spotlight suggestions from appearing there.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces promotional or suggestion-style notifications, but it can also remove helpful Microsoft guidance that would otherwise appear in that area.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter, while CloudContent.admx maps the user Group Policy to DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the Action Center Spotlight policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Action Center Spotlight allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Spotlight content can appear in that notification surface.

allowed_values.label: Action Center Spotlight disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Spotlight content is blocked in that notification surface by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter = 1, which matches the documented Group Policy disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1703 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents the allow-form default as allowed, while the registry policy baseline remains not configured unless the policy is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who are fine with Spotlight content in notification surfaces
avoid_for: Users who want fewer prompts in notification areas
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its ordinary Action Center Spotlight behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want fewer suggestion-style prompts in notification surfaces.
tradeoffs: No policy-based restriction is enforced for this surface.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet UI
avoid_for: Users who want Microsoft guidance in notification areas

states.rationale: Turn off Spotlight content in Action Center through Group Policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want fewer Spotlight suggestions appearing in notification surfaces.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to continue surfacing guidance in that area.
tradeoffs: Action Center style Spotlight prompts are removed.
Some Microsoft tips or suggestions in that surface disappear.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AllowWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the allow-form policy controlling Windows Spotlight content in Action Center style notification surfaces.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user Group Policy to DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft CloudContent policy files document the exact user-policy surface, numeric mapping, and Action Center behavior. The app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent" valueName="DisableWindowsSpotlightOnActionCenter">; CloudContent.adml: If you enable this policy, Windows Spotlight notifications will no longer be shown on Action Center.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy removes Windows Spotlight notifications from Action Center.
